title: Sebastian Sattler: Plagiate in Hausarbeiten. Erklarungsmodelle mit Hilfe der Rational Choice Theorie.
abstract: Plagiate in Hausarbeiten. Erklärungsmodelle mit Hilfe der Rational Choice Theorie [Plagiarism in Seminar Theses. Explanations Based on Rational Choice Theory] deals with the prevalence of plagiarism in university students’ seminar theses and with the question of which factors explain their susceptibility to this kind of deviant behaviour. This is an important research topic because plagiarism represents a serious threat for the institution of intellectual property in academia. Furthermore, it undermines the validity of the information provided by university certificates about the qualification of their holders. Thus, we would like to know the conditions under which plagiarism is more or less likely to occur in order to take efficient counter measures for preventing academic dishonesty. However, research on this topic is rather restricted. Sattler has written a book which provides a comprehensive overview over the nonetheless available knowledge about the prevalence and antecedent conditions of plagiarism of university students. The book offers a rigorously formulated theoretical basis for understanding, explaining, and predicting those conditions under which students are expected to violate norms of academic honesty. Sattler utilizes self-collected data from sociology students at a German university and their academic teachers in order to test the theoretical hypotheses which have been obtained in a previous step.
